item.description:
ChainLink 0.3.668

A small follow-up to 0.3.665.

  • Fixed duplicate events. An event could appear twice in Upcoming Events after a sync — the duplicate now removes itself on launch, and it can't happen again.
  • The training plan's "longest ride" stat now says what it is — your longest recent ride — and shows the date it happened.
  • Career Bests load instantly after the first visit instead of re-reading your whole history every time.
  • Includes everything from 0.3.665 — the fitness signature that follows your training, breakthrough badges, career bests, and zoom-scoped graph averages.

A note about Strava

As of 30 June 2026, Strava requires an active Strava subscription for the API access ChainLink's Strava connection uses. If your subscription has lapsed, the connection will stop working — that's Strava's change, not a ChainLink bug, and the app now says so plainly instead of showing "Connected" next to an error. If you sync through Garmin Connect you're unaffected, and it remains the recommended way to get rides in.

ChainLink 0.3.665

Everything from 0.3.663, plus two additions to the ride graph and your records.

New since 0.3.663

  • Zoom the performance graph and the averages follow. Drag to select a climb, a sprint or an interval and the legend reports the averages for that section — power, heart rate, speed, elevation — rather than the whole ride. The selection is tinted and labelled with its length so it can't be mistaken for the ride average. Reset to go back to the full ride.
  • Career Bests are clickable. Each record now names the ride that set it and opens straight to it, so you can check a number rather than take it on trust.

And everything from 0.3.663

  • Your fitness signature now goes down as well as up. It follows your training load rather than remembering a good day forever — keep training and it holds, ease off and it drifts, and a hard effort pushes it back up. Each energy system moves at its own pace.
  • Breakthrough badges on rides that genuinely raised your fitness, a Breakthroughs page under Fitness showing what each one moved, and a Career Bests record that never decays.
  • The same numbers on every device, worked out from your rides rather than copied between them — and no Mac required.
  • Dodgy power spikes from a dying battery or sensor glitch no longer pollute your power curve or peak power.
  • If anything ever looks wrong: Settings → Maintenance → Recompute fitness data. Safe, nothing is deleted, running it twice changes nothing.

ChainLink 0.3.663

Your fitness numbers now describe today — and they agree on every device.

This is a big one. If you have ever looked at your threshold and thought "that isn't me any more", this release is the fix.

Your fitness signature now changes with your training

  • It goes down as well as up. Previously your threshold, matchbook and peak power could only ever rise — a single great ride years ago set a number that never came back down. Now they follow your training load: keep training and they hold, ease off and they drift, and a hard effort pushes them back up.
  • Each energy system moves at its own pace, so a block of long steady miles keeps your endurance while your sharp edge fades — which is what actually happens.
  • All three numbers are now measured from your own riding rather than estimated from a starting guess.

Breakthroughs

  • Rides that genuinely raised your fitness now get a gold, silver or bronze badge in your ride list, and there is a new Breakthroughs page under Fitness showing what each one moved.
  • A new Career Bests card records your best ever effort at each duration. These never decay — they are a record of what you have done, kept separate from what you can do today.

The same numbers everywhere

  • Your Mac, iPhone and iPad now show identical fitness numbers. They are worked out from your rides on each device rather than copied between them, so they cannot drift apart.
  • You no longer need a Mac. Ride detail and fitness analysis work the same on an iPhone or iPad alone.
  • If your numbers ever look wrong: Settings → Maintenance → Recompute fitness data. It is safe, nothing is deleted, and running it twice changes nothing.

Fixes

  • Dodgy power readings — the spikes you get from a dying battery or a sensor glitch — no longer pollute your power curve or peak power.
  • The coach no longer describes an old peak as your current fitness.
  • Faster, more accurate ride detail on phones and iPads.
  • Various layout fixes on iPhone.

Coming from 0.3.628? This includes everything since — quite a lot.
